key_points: Acknowledged housing crisis driven by over-admitting students
Off-campus complex UClub had secretive management changes, declining standards
Oxford affordable housing shortage affects students and locals alike
key_quotes: Ole Miss doesn't care, they're the ones causing it by admitting everyone with a pulse.
The school gets paid based on the number they accept... from the state's perspective.
Thankful my son is able to live at home with me while he is at school here.

key_points: Grove tailgating ranked #1 nationally — legitimately world-class party scene
Party culture so dominant that student section sits at quarter capacity for games
Greek life massive and central; essentially defines the social experience here
key_quotes: This is the double-edged sword of having the best tailgating and best party scene in all of America.
Greek Life is massive in the South... Ole Miss, Bama, FSU, Clemson are Greek dominated.
We took our LSU friends to tailgate in the Grove... They were blown away... there needs to be some way to note how much of a gap there is going from #1 to #2.

key_points: No meaningful student dining data surfaced in available posts
Oxford Square has bar and restaurant scene students genuinely enjoy
The Library bar ranked #7 in top college bars nationally
key_quotes: The Library at Ole Miss ranked #7 in the Top 25 College Bars in the U.S.
Kroger is the only grocery store that's open [during the storm].

key_points: One student took a semester off for mental health, graduated years late
Journalism major described as actively harmful to mental wellbeing
No meaningful support infrastructure mentioned by any student
key_quotes: Due to several personal reasons, I've struggled with my mental health while at university. I took spring semester off because of it.
I changed my major from journalism to Multidisciplinary Studies because I felt the negative impact the journalism major was having on me.
4-Year University Has Drained Me.

key_points: Automatic merit scholarships for OOS students are a genuine draw — well documented
Need-based aid is weak; in-state students often get little to nothing
Full tuition scholarships available for 32+ ACT / 3.5+ GPA out-of-state applicants
key_quotes: Ole Miss — still has merit money too!
Ole Miss is the only college that I could definitely afford without my parents taking loans.
Ole Miss is offering me practically no financial aid [in-state student].

key_points: Engineering program specifically called out as weak — labs and facilities lacking
Croft Institute for International Studies praised as a hidden gem program
Easy to coast academically; known as a school that admits broadly with low bar
key_quotes: The engineering school here isn't that good. From what I've seen, the labs and facilities are really lacking.
Ole Miss does something similar with their Croft Institute... are there any other schools with programs similar to these?
My college counselor said they'll be accepting kids with 2.0s and 4.0s.

key_points: Housing crisis petition ignored; admin accused of over-admitting for state funding
Infrastructure spending priorities questioned — landscaping over utilities
Power outage response criticized; students told to leave for 1-4 weeks
key_quotes: Having lived in Oxford for 8 years I can easily say that the town needs better leadership and infrastructure expenditure. They can spend millions on landscaping and football but can't spend 1/10 of that on power infrastructure.
Ole Miss doesn't care, they're the ones causing it by admitting everyone with a pulse.
Perhaps lower the acceptance rate like every other school in the conference.

key_points: Oxford is a genuinely charming, walkable small college town students love
The Grove is a legitimately iconic campus space — not just marketing
Small town means limited infrastructure; one ice storm exposed serious fragility
key_quotes: I get comments on my OM gear all over the world. I was in the airport in Frankfurt Germany last month and got a loud HOTTY TODDY!
We welcome everyone at Oxford.
We have no power, water, food, gas or ac [during winter storm].

key_points: Engineering and STEM outcomes weak relative to peer SEC schools
Law and business networking reasonable given SEC alumni base
Widely used as a scholarship safety school, not a career-forward choice
key_quotes: I want to go to law school so minimizing undergraduate debt is very important to me.
The engineering school here isn't that good.
I got into Clemson, Alabama, LSU, and Ole Miss... top three are LSU, Georgia, and Florida.

key_points: Outstanding value for OOS students with strong test scores — hard to beat
In-state students get less aid and pay more relative to what they receive
Multiple students explicitly chose Ole Miss to avoid loan debt elsewhere
key_quotes: Ole Miss is the only college that I could definitely afford without my parents taking loans.
Full ride to Ole Miss vs. Brown University for Mechanical Engineering + Medicine.
Ole Miss — still has merit money too!

key_points: Fun, party-forward school with genuine school spirit and beloved traditions
Football culture dominates campus identity — for better and worse
Warm, welcoming community; strangers bond over Hotty Toddy across the country
key_quotes: Hotty fucking Toddy!
I chose Ole Miss since Notre Dame doesn't have Greek life and I prioritize the college experience.
I could pick up some mustard bottles and Titleist golf balls for when he comes back to Vaught-Hemingway.

key_points: **Housing:** Acknowledged student housing crisis from deliberate over-enrollment
**Safety:** Freshman football player killed in shooting; LGBTQ+ alum murder case on record
**Culture:** Fan used homophobic slurs repeatedly at game; frat boys called out for consistent bad behavior
key_quotes: Ole Miss freshman football player Corey Adams killed in shooting.
One gentleman behind me had consistent use of 'fag' to describe any bad play... ruining her experience [child present].
I wholeheartedly apologize for the Greek tragedy forever taking place on our campus.

key_points: Croft Institute for International Studies is a genuinely distinctive program
Stamps Scholarship offers full ride + enrichment funding — nationally competitive
Oxford Square bar and restaurant scene gives school a real college-town identity
key_quotes: Ole Miss does something similar with their Croft Institute... are there any other schools with programs similar to these?
I have received a full ride at Ole Miss through the Stamps Scholarship program and their honors college.
The Library at Ole Miss ranked #7 in the Top 25 College Bars in the U.S.
